stories.title: Mariners Win 7 Straight — Cole Young Walk-Off Single, Third Walk-Off in 4 Days

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Cole Young. Broken-bat single. 10th inning. Walk-off.

Seattle wins 3-2. SEVEN straight. Third walk-off in four games. 🔥

The Mariners are real. 32-29, three games over .500, surging. ⚾

#Mariners #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Julio Rodriguez hit 10 home runs in May. Best month of his career. 🔥

He went from "what happened to that guy" to AL MVP conversation.

The Mariners are walking off games left and right. Something is happening in Seattle. ⚾

#Mariners #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Monday night in Seattle, Cole Young came up to the plate in the bottom of the 10th with the game tied at 2. With Randy Arozarena on third after stealing his way there, Young hit a broken-bat single into left field. The game was over.

Three walk-off wins in four days. Seven consecutive victories. The Mariners are 32-29 and three games over .500 — the best mark of their season.

Emerson Hancock was the backbone of Monday's win, going six strong innings and allowing only two hits (both solo home runs). The bullpen — Ferrer, Brash, and Muñoz — handled the rest without drama.

But the real story might be Julio Rodriguez. He hit 10 home runs in May — the best single month of his career. The guy who came up with enormous expectations and hit a speed bump is back to being exactly what everyone thought he could become.

The Mariners didn't look like a playoff team in April. Check back in July. ⚾

What's your AL West prediction for the rest of the season? Drop it below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: Cole Young. Broken bat. Walk-off single. Seven straight. Seattle is surging. 🔥 #Mariners #MLB

article.headline: Mariners Win Seven Straight — Cole Young's Walk-Off Caps Third Dramatic Finish in Four Days

stories.title: Elly De La Cruz IL — Grade 1-2 Hamstring Strain, Reds Call Up Edwin Arroyo for MLB Debut

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
BREAKING: Elly De La Cruz is on the IL. 🚨

Grade 1-2 right hamstring strain. Expected to miss 2-4 weeks.

He was hitting .280 with 12 HRs and 37 RBI. MVP-caliber numbers, gone in an instant.

#Reds #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Reds lose De La Cruz, call up Edwin Arroyo for his MLB debut. 👀

The Reds are 6 back in the NL Central. They can't afford to be without their best player.

But baseball has a way of finding new heroes right when you need one. ⚾

#Reds #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The play looked scary the moment it happened. Elly De La Cruz pulled up after reaching first base, signaling something was wrong before he even took his first step. The MRI confirmed it: a Grade 1-2 right hamstring strain.

The timeline is 2-4 weeks. For the Reds, who are already six games behind Milwaukee in the NL Central, that's a long time to play without their best player.

De La Cruz was having a season that had people seriously talking MVP: .280 average, .855 OPS, 12 home runs, 37 RBI. He is one of the most electric players in the sport — the kind of guy who changes a game just by being on first base.

In his absence, Cincinnati called up Edwin Arroyo for his MLB debut. Arroyo gets his moment, and the organization gets to see what one of their top prospects is made of. Baseball has a way of finding those stories when you least expect them.

But the Reds need Elly back. And they need him healthy.

Your thoughts on the Reds' playoff chances while De La Cruz is out? 👇
facebook.image_caption: Elly De La Cruz heads to the IL with a hamstring strain. Top prospect Edwin Arroyo is on his way up. 🚨 #Reds #MLB

stories.title: Schwarber's Record Chase — Now at 22 HRs, On Pace for 66

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber: 22 home runs. MLB lead. On pace for 66. 💥

Only McGwire and Bonds had more through this many games.

We might be watching the greatest HR chase of the modern era unfold in Philadelphia. ⚾

#Phillies #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Kyle Schwarber has 22 home runs. The season started in late March. Let that math work itself out for a second.

The Philadelphia Phillies slugger leads all of MLB and is on a pace — through roughly 55 games — that projects to 66 home runs for the full season. That number hasn't been reached by a clean player in modern baseball. The last players with more through 49 games were Mark McGwire in 1998 and Barry Bonds in 2001, who finished with 70 and 73, respectively.

Schwarber is the 18th player in MLB history with at least 20 home runs through a team's first 49 games. The last one was Josh Hamilton in 2012.

To put it plainly: what he is doing right now has almost never been done. And he's showing no signs of slowing down.

Does this end with 60 home runs? 65? More? We don't know. But it is the story of the MLB season, and right now, Kyle Schwarber owns it. 💥

Can Schwarber hit 60 this year? Vote in the comments! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: 22 home runs. On pace for 66. Kyle Schwarber vs. history. 💥 #Phillies #MLB

stories.title: Tigers Rotation Recovery — Skubal and Verlander Both Making Rehab Starts

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Detroit Tigers rotation update:

⚾ Tarik Skubal — rehab start possible this Saturday
⚾ Justin Verlander — rehabbing at Triple-A Toledo today
⚾ Casey Mize — still on IL

That's two Cy Young contenders getting closer to return. The AL gets more interesting. ⚾

#Tigers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
This time last month, the Detroit Tigers' rotation looked like a hospital ward.

Tarik Skubal — one of the best pitchers in the AL — had undergone NanoScope surgery to remove loose bodies from his elbow and hadn't pitched in a real game since April 29. Justin Verlander, 43 years old and making yet another comeback, had thrown just one start all season before left hip inflammation shut him down again. Casey Mize? Still out.

Monday brings some good news. Skubal is expected to make a rehab start against Triple-A opponents as soon as this Saturday, tracking for a mid/late-June return to the big club. Verlander is rehabbing at Triple-A Toledo today, testing his hip in a live game setting.

Neither return is guaranteed. But the fact that both are on a rehab progression — making real outings, throwing real pitches — is the first sign in weeks that Detroit's rotation might be whole again before the summer is out.

A healthy Skubal changes things. Significantly. ⚾

Do you think the Tigers can still contend this season? Tell us below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: Skubal nearing a rehab start. Verlander rehabbing in Triple-A. The Tigers rotation is coming back to life. ⚾ #Tigers #MLB

stories.title: Braves Are Baseball's Best — 36-16, Nine-Game NL East Lead, Acuña Returns to Form

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The Atlanta Braves are 36-16. Nine-game lead in the NL East. 🔥

Acuña hitting grand slams. Sale striking out 8 in his sleep.

At what point do we just accept this is the NL's team to beat in October? ⚾

#Braves #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The Atlanta Braves are 36-16. They lead the NL East by roughly nine games. They have 18 comeback wins. Ronald Acuña Jr. is hitting grand slams. Chris Sale is striking out eight batters against his former team like it's still 2020.

At some point, a lead is no longer a race. The NL East is getting close to that point.

Atlanta is balanced in a way that very few teams achieve: elite starting pitching, a deep and reliable bullpen, a lineup that can score from anywhere, and the kind of team culture that finds late-game wins when the game should already be lost. The 18 comeback victories are the most impressive number in that list. Any team can win when they're ahead. This one keeps winning when they're behind.

The Braves aren't just the NL's best team right now. They're building a case to be the World Series favorite, full stop.

Who beats the Braves in the playoffs? We genuinely want to know. 👇
facebook.image_caption: 36-16. Nine-game lead. The Braves are building the best record in baseball. 🔥 #Braves #MLB

article.headline: Braves Are 36-16 With a Nine-Game NL East Lead — When Does the Division Race End?
